domain:
In an academic institution, students enroll in various courses offered by the university. Each student is uniquely identified by a student code. An enrollment in the course requires the specific date on which it occurred for administrative purposes.

Courses are supported by Teaching Assistants (TAs), who are identified by their first and last names. A TA may assist in multiple different courses, and the specific role of a TA within a course is defined by an assignment, which describes the details and responsibilities assigned to them. Regarding courses logistics, they consist of one or more lab sections. Each section occurs at a specific location and time slot with start and end times.

questions.question:
Should details be modeled as a separate class associated with Assignment, or remain as a simple representation?

For example:
1. Details is a separate class with its own attributes. This allows users to query and filter by individual details properties.
2. Details is modeled as a simple representation in Assignment. This is suitable when details details are not needed.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: The Details is a separate class with its own attributes.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Model details as a simple representation in Assignment.
questions.selected: Option 1: The Details is a separate class with its own attributes.

questions.question:
We know that each assignment has a responsibilities. When we think about responsibilities, is it just a simple piece of information, like a line of text? 
Or is responsibilities made up of different parts that might be important?
For example:
1. For each responsibilities, the detail information is saved separately, so users can filter assignment by detail.
2. The responsibilities is a single piece of text. This works well when we just need to show the responsibilities.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: The Responsibilities is a separate class with its own attributes.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Model responsibilities as a simple representation in Assignment.
questions.selected: Option 1: We need the responsibilities details separately.

questions.question:
Should the relationship between Course and Assignment be modeled as a composition or as an association?

For example:
1. Use composition, Assignment would depend on Course for its existence.
2. Use association, Assignment could exist independently of Course, and can also be linked to multiple courses.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Composition is needed because class Assignment depends on Course.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Association is sufficient because class Assignment exists independently of Course.
questions.selected: Option 1: Composition is needed because class Assignment depends on Course.

questions.question:
Should the relationship between Assignment and Assignment be modeled as a composition or as an association?

For example:
1. Use composition, Assignment would depend on Assignment for its existence.
2. Use association, Assignment could exist independently of Assignment, and can also be linked to multiple assignments.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Composition is needed because class Assignment depends on Assignment.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Association is sufficient because class Assignment exists independently of Assignment.
questions.selected: Option 2: Association is sufficient because class Assignment exists independently of Assignment.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Course in the relationship with Assignment?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, an assignment can exist without any associated course.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, an assignment must have at least one associated course.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Assignment can exist without Course.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, Assignment must have at least one Course.
questions.selected: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, Assignment must have at least one Course.

questions.question:
Should description be modeled as a separate class associated with Role, or remain as a simple representation?

For example:
1. Description is a separate class with its own attributes. This allows users to query and filter by individual description properties.
2. Description is modeled as a simple representation in Role. This is suitable when description details are not needed.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: The Description is a separate class with its own attributes.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Model description as a simple representation in Role.
questions.selected: Option 2: Model description as a simple representation in Role.

questions.question:
Should the description in role be modeled as a an attribute which allows free text input, or as an enumeration with predefined literals?

For example:
1. Use description as string attribute in role, which allows any text value.
2. Use description as an enumeration which uses predefined values in literals: grader, lab instructor, course assistant or tutor.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: String attribute that allows any text value for description.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Enumeration description with predefined values: literal 1, literal 2 and literal 3.
questions.selected: Option 1: String attribute that allows any text value for description.

questions.question:
Should the relationship between Assignment and Role be modeled as a composition or as an association?

For example:
1. Use composition, Role would depend on Assignment for its existence.
2. Use association, Role could exist independently of Assignment, and can also be linked to multiple assignments.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Composition is needed because class Role depends on Assignment.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Association is sufficient because class Role exists independently of Assignment.
questions.selected: Option 2: Association is sufficient because class Role exists independently of Assignment.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Assignment in the relationship with Role?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, a role can exist without any associated assignment.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, a role must have at least one associated assignment.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Role can exist without Assignment.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, Role must have at least one Assignment.
questions.selected: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Role can exist without Assignment.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Role in the relationship with Assignment?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, an assignment can exist without any associated role.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, an assignment must have at least one associated role.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Assignment can exist without Role.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,Assignment must have at least one Role.
questions.selected: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Assignment can exist without Role.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of TeachingAssistant in the relationship with Assignment?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, an assignment can exist without any associated teaching assistant.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, an assignment must have at least one associated teaching assistant.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Assignment can exist without TeachingAssistant.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, Assignment must have at least one TeachingAssistant.
questions.selected: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Assignment can exist without TeachingAssistant.

questions.question:
What is the maximum cardinality of Assignment in the relationship with TeachingAssistant?

For example:
1. Maximum cardinality of one, a teaching assistant is associated with exactly one assignment.
2. Maximum cardinality of many, a teaching assistant can be associated with multiple assignments.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: One as maximun cardinality, each teaching assistant links to one assignment.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each teaching assistant can link to multiple assignments.
questions.selected: Option 1: One as maximun cardinality, each teaching assistant links to one assignment.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Course in the relationship with Enrollment?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, an enrollment can exist without any associated course.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, an enrollment must have at least one associated course.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Enrollment can exist without Course.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, Enrollment must have at least one Course.
questions.selected: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Enrollment can exist without Course.

questions.question:
Should the relationship between Student and Enrollment be modeled as a composition or as an association?

For example:
1. Use composition, Enrollment would depend on Student for its existence.
2. Use association, Enrollment could exist independently of Student, and can also be linked to multiple students.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Composition is needed because class Enrollment depends on Student.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Association is sufficient because class Enrollment exists independently of Student.
questions.selected: Option 2: Association is sufficient because class Enrollment exists independently of Student.

questions.question:
Should time slot be modeled as a separate class associated with LabSection, or as a simple representation within LabSection?

For example:
1. Time slot is a separate class with attributes: start time and end time. This allows users to query and filter by individual time slot properties.
2. Time slot is modeled as a simple represntation in LabSection. This is suitable when time slot details are not needed.
Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: The TimeSlot is a separate class with its own attributes.
questions.option_2: Option 2: The time slot is a simple representation in LabSection.
questions.selected: Option 2: The time slot is a simple representation in LabSection.

questions.question:
Should Enrollment be modeled as an association class linking Student and Course, or as a regular class with two separate associations?

For example:
1. Association class: Enrollment exists only as part of the relationship between Student and Course. A enrollment cannot exist without the link between Student and Course.
2. Regular class: Enrollment is a class that has links to Student and Course. In this case multiple enrollments can exist for every Student and Course.


Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Association class, Enrollment as association class linking Student and Course.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Regular class, Enrollment as separate class with two associations.
questions.selected: Option 1: Association class, Enrollment as association class linking Student and Course.
